name: Pork Chop Suey
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Lean pork; cut into 1/4-inch-wide strips
White rice and/or chow mein noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork into no more than 1/4 inch strips so it will be well cooked, but do not cook longer than the time given for the finest chop suey.
2. Sauté pork in hot fat for 5 minutes until delicately browned.
3. Add celery and onions and cook an additional 2-3 minutes until slightly softened.
4. Stir in salt and boiling water; cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
5. Mix molasses, soy sauce, and flour blended with half a cup of water.
6. Cook the mixture until it boils, stirring constantly.
7. Add the drained chop suey vegetables and continue cooking until thoroughly heated.
8. Serve with hot fluffy rice and/or chow mein noodles and additional soy sauce if desired.
Nutritional info per serving: 249 cal; 14g pro, 26g carb, 11g fat (37%).
